content:
## Notice Timing
Customers must give Northstar Bank notice of a credit-card billing error within 60 days. Treat the timing question as a threshold issue before discussing provisional credit. If the customer is describing a transaction dispute but the notice timing is unclear, ask for the statement or transaction timing needed to determine whether the notice is timely.

See "Credit Card Billing Error Intake" for intake details and "Credit Dispute Intake Quick Reference" for the shorter agent checklist.

## When Provisional Credit Can Be Discussed
Provisional credit is available only when all required conditions are present: transaction_status_is_posted, notice_is_timely, transaction_not_already_disputed, resolution_request_is_valid, dispute_amount_is_valid, reason_allows_provisional_credit. Do not describe provisional credit as automatic for every accepted dispute. The transaction must already be posted, the notice must be timely, the same transaction must not already be disputed, the requested resolution and amount must be valid, and the reason must fall within the allowed reason set.

The allowed provisional-credit reasons are: unauthorized_fraudulent_charge, duplicate_charge, incorrect_amount, goods_services_not_received, canceled_subscription_still_charging, refund_never_processed.

## Quality Dispute Exception
A dispute reason of goods_services_not_as_described is handled differently. For this synthetic policy, accepted goods/services quality disputes can be filed but do not receive provisional credit automatically. If a customer reports that goods or services were not as described, explain that the dispute may still be filed when otherwise accepted, but do not promise provisional credit under this policy. For the full provisional-credit handling standard, see "Credit Card Provisional Credit Policy".
title: Credit Dispute Timing FAQ
